summary: Tested Crew Management — Services catalog (backend + frontend). 16/16 pytest cases pass including CRUD, tenant-isolation, seed-defaults idempotency, industries list, job/visit service linking, enrichment + fallback logic, and critical public-tracker leak check. Frontend screenshots confirm Services nav, seeded list (7), color-coded Jobs Service column + inline dropdown, color-coded Schedule visit cards, and VisitModal Service dropdown work.
critical_code_review_comments: Services modal on ServicesPage lacks a data-testid attribute on the name input; tests had to rely on placeholder/name heuristics. Recommend adding data-testid on all modal inputs (services-modal-name-input, services-modal-save-btn, etc.).
Jobs page table cells likely use div-based rows rather than <th>; adding data-testid='jobs-service-select' on the inline service <select> would make automation more robust.

cm_services.py create/update catches 'duplicate key' via string matching on the exception message — brittle. Prefer catching postgrest.exceptions.APIError and inspecting its code (e.g., '23505') for uniqueness violations.
seed_data_creation: Test-created services cleaned up via test_zz_cleanup_created_services. Jobs/visits created mid-test are deleted in their own tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite is at /app/backend/tests/test_cm_services.py. Covers: login, list services (active + include_inactive), industries list, create, duplicate 400, update, soft-delete, hard-delete-rejected-when-referenced, seed-defaults idempotent, jobs enriched with service_name/color, create-job-with-service + patch + clear (service_id=null), visit enrichment fallback from parent job (critical), cross-tenant 404, and public tracker leak check. Job status enum: {draft, scheduled, in_progress, completed, cancelled} — visit status enum: {scheduled, on_site, completed, cancelled}. Public tracker (GET /api/cm/track/{token}) correctly omits all service_* fields per requirement 5a.
rca_of_the_issue: No issues found. All 31 features listed in review_request were either automated-tested (backend) or visually verified (frontend screenshots). Initial pytest run had 2 failures due to test using wrong job status value 'new' — tests updated to use 'draft'; not a product bug.
